backend.changelog: Co-release with CadBridge 1.8.85 — a wedged (frozen-COM) SolidWorks at the start of Generate is auto-recovered with a one-click restart prompt; a large assembly that's merely still loading is NOT mistaken for a wedge.

cadBridge.changelog: If SolidWorks is truly frozen (COM not responding AND the process idle) when you click Generate & Export, the app detects it, asks you to save any work, and restarts SolidWorks into a clean session automatically — no 'Cannot verify SolidWorks state' dead-end, no manual kill, and your unsaved work is never discarded without asking. A very large assembly that is simply still loading (CPU/memory active) is correctly treated as healthy and is NOT restarted.
